narrative: OMX30 faces a challenging open as European peers (DAX -0.73%, CAC -0.68%) post overnight declines and global correlation deteriorates to -0.448%. The index maintains structural strength (still +7.6% above 200-MA, ROC-90 +8.0%), but technical conviction is fragile: ADX at 12.3 (below 18 threshold) requires confidence reduction per rules, and RSI at 36.31 approaches oversold levels prone to mean reversion whipsaws. The 83bp rise in US 10Y yields benefits banks (NIM expansion) but pressures real estate refinancing costs and technology DCF multiples. Commodity divergence—copper rallying sharply (+2.82%) while oil flat—creates mixed input-cost signals for industrials. UK political turmoil adds regional anxiety without direct OMX catalyst.
morningBrief: Open cautiously neutral; weak ADX and approaching oversold RSI reduce confidence to Low with 30% magnitude cut applied. Expect range-bound trade near 3123 (yesterday close) with support at 3085 (lower Bollinger) and resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Banks (SEB A, Handelsbanken A) gain modest traction on higher yields (+83bp), but real estate (Balder, Castellum) faces headwind from refinancing cost pressures. Industrials mixed: Boliden strongly bid on copper +2.82%, but Volvo/Atlas Copco sideways as weak ADX clouds direction. Tech/Telecom (Ericsson, Hexagon) at risk on NASDAQ weakness (-0.13%) and yield compression concerns. Watch for US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET as afternoon catalyst. Risk-off tone favours defensive rotation into AstraZeneca/Essity if sentiment deteriorates further.
drivers: Global equity weakness (-0.45% weighted correlation signal) with DAX/CAC leading declines
Weak ADX (12.3) reduces conviction; RSI 36.31 approaching oversold territory signals mean reversion risk
Copper strength (+2.82%) offsets oil stability, but yields up 83bp constrains tech/real estate valuations
UK political uncertainty (Labour losses, Reform surge) creates regional headwind for pan-European sentiment

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +83bp to 4.39% expands NIM outlook; Swedish mortgage spreads benefit.
Rising yields (+83bp) increase refinancing and discount rate pressures on property valuations.
NASDAQ -0.13%, yield rise compresses DCF multiples, MACD below signal weakens momentum.
Copper +2.82% and Gold +0.75% drive direct revenue lift; direct commodity price exposure.
Mixed signals: copper strength offsets flat oil, weak ADX limits conviction, USD/SEK flat.
EUR/SEK flat (-0.28%); sourcing cost pressures muted. Oil flat caps spending headwind.

watchList.reason: Copper +2.82% and gold +0.75% provide strong near-term tailwind; highest conviction sector call.
US yield +83bp supports NIM expansion; classic rate-up beneficiary with secular appeal.
NASDAQ weakness, yield headwinds, and MACD below signal create near-term downside bias.
Real estate valuation compression from +83bp yield move; refinancing cost spike near-term drag.
Weak ADX (12.3) and RSI 36.31 (near oversold) suggest consolidation; mean reversion risk high.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish releases. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET (key afternoon catalyst). UK local election results ongoing (low market impact but adds to risk-off sentiment if Labour losses extend).
topRisk: Global correlation weakness persists; DAX/CAC continued slide could trigger stop-loss cascade in OMX30 below 3085. US Jobless Claims surprise (either direction) could spike VIX and unwound positioning. Escalating UK political uncertainty (Reform surge) adds tail risk to risk appetite.
technicalSummary: Still +7.6% above 200-MA (structural bull intact) but +0.6% above 50-MA shows momentum fading
MACD below signal line with negative histogram (-3.147) flags bearish crossover risk
RSI 36.31 approaching oversold (<30); mean reversion bounce possible but not high conviction
ADX 12.3 is weak (below 18 threshold) — reduce all confidence calls by 30% per rules
Stochastic K=39.2, D=61.7 neutral; no overbought/oversold extremes in oscillators
Bollinger %B=0.29 in lower half signals potential support bounce, but width 5.17% is narrow (low volatility environment)
